title: hieracium murorum subsp. distractum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. distractum, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hawkweed plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It is native to Europe and Western Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, likely due to its flavonoid and sesquiterpene content. Herbal preparations of Hieracium murorum subsp. distractum can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
